core_mechanism: a holographic bulk-boundary duality: a strongly-coupled system's linear-response transport and slow relaxational (hydrodynamic) modes are computed from a higher-dimensional gravitational background with a horizon, where transport coefficients follow from the low-frequency/low-momentum limit of correlation functions and the collective damped modes appear as the horizon's low-lying quasinormal (decaying) eigenmodes
brief_reason: The abstract builds its whole method on the AdS/CFT correspondence, computing field-theory correlators from a black p-brane geometry and matching hydrodynamic modes to the black hole's low-lying quasinormal modes.
